changes_made: Changed k8s_audit_log_time_stamps_configured to k8s_audit_log_timestamps_configured for consistency in naming
review_notes: The control AU-8 is applicable to Kubernetes as it pertains to audit logging and the inclusion of timestamps, which are critical for audit trails. The function names generally adhere to the naming conventions, but a minor change was made for consistency in the spelling of 'timestamps'. All functions map correctly to Kubernetes audit logging capabilities.
naming_improvements: Ensure consistent spelling of 'timestamps' across all functions for clarity.
title: Time Stamps
step1_reasoning: The AU-8 control focuses on ensuring that time stamps are included in audit logs, which is directly applicable to Kubernetes audit logging. Kubernetes can be configured to include time stamps in its audit logs, and ensuring audit logging is enabled is a fundamental aspect of meeting this control. Additionally, configuring log retention is important for maintaining historical data, which aligns with the intent of the control.
